translations.idiomatic: Ministers instead sent a tactful letter that loosened the knot and calmed the realm.’

sentences.zh: 及孝文時，匈奴侵暴北邊，候騎至雍甘泉，京師大駭，發三將軍屯細柳、棘門、霸上以備之，數月乃罷。

translations.literal: Reaching Emperor Xiaowen’s time, Xiongnu invaded brutally northern border; scout riders reached Yong-Ganquan; capital greatly alarmed; issued three generals encamp at Xiliu, Jimen, Bashang to guard against them—several months then dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wen raiders reached Yong and Ganquan; Chang’an mobilized three armies at Xiliu, Jimen, and Bashang for months on end.

sentences.zh: 孝武即位，設馬邑之權，欲誘匈奴，使韓安國將三十萬眾徼於便地，匈奴覺之而去，徒費財勞師，一虜不可得見，況單于之面乎！

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaowu ascended, set Mayi stratagem, wished lure Xiongnu; ordered Han Anguo lead three hundred thousand masses patrol advantageous ground; Xiongnu perceived it and departed—in vain expense wealth weary armies; one captive not able obtain see—how much more the Chanyu’s face!
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu’s Mayi ambush wasted treasure and troops without netting even a scout—let alone the Chanyu’s face!

sentences.zh: 其後深惟社稷之計，規恢萬載之策，乃大興師數十萬，使衛青、霍去病操兵，前後十餘年。

translations.literal: Afterward deeply pondered altars of soil and grain’s plans, planned restore ten thousand generations’ strategy; then greatly raised armies several hundred thousand, ordered Wei Qing, Huo Qubing wield arms—before and after more than ten years.
translations.idiomatic: Then he weighed the altars of state and poured hundreds of thousands into Wei Qing and Huo Qubing offensives lasting more than a decade.

sentences.zh: 於是浮西河，絕大幕，破寘顏，襲王庭，窮極其地，追奔逐北，封狼居胥山，禪於姑衍，以臨翰海，虜名王貴人以百數。

translations.literal: Thereupon floated West River, crossed great desert, broke Zhiyan, raided royal court, exhausted their lands, pursued fleeing chased north, enfeoffed Wolf-Ju-xu Mountain, performed Chan at Guyang, gazed on Han Sea—captured famous kings nobles by hundreds count.
translations.idiomatic: They crossed the Yellow River and Gobi, smashed Zhiyan, stormed the royal headquarters, pursued fugitives to Wolf-Ju-xu and Guyang, overlooked the northern sea, and bagged hundreds of named nobles.

sentences.zh: 自是之後，匈奴震怖，益求和親，然而未肯稱臣也。

translations.literal: From this afterward, Xiongnu trembled fear; increasingly sought peace kinship—yet not willing declare subject.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the Xiongnu trembled and sued harder for heqin marriages yet still refused to call themselves subjects.

sentences.zh: 且夫前世豈樂傾無量之費，役無罪之人，快心於狼望之北哉？

translations.literal: Moreover former ages how gladly pour measureless expense, conscript guiltless people, satisfy heart north of Langwang?
translations.idiomatic: Did past dynasties relish bankrupting the treasury and drafting innocent men for sport beyond Wolf Outlook?

sentences.zh: 以為不一勞者不久佚，不暫費者不永寧，是以忍百萬之師以摧餓虎之喙，運府庫之財填盧山之壑而不悔也。

translations.literal: Thinking that without one toil there is not long ease; without temporary expense there is not eternal peace—therefore endured million armies to smash starving tiger’s beak; transported treasury wealth to fill Lushan’s ravines without regret.
translations.idiomatic: They believed ease follows strain and peace follows price—so they threw million-strong hosts against a starving tiger’s jaws and emptied granaries into Lushan’s gorges without regret.

sentences.zh: 至本始之初，匈奴有桀心，欲掠烏孫，侵公主，乃發五將之師十五萬騎獵其南，而長羅侯以烏孫五萬騎震其西，皆至質而還。

translations.literal: Reaching Benshi’s beginning, Xiongnu had fierce heart, wished plunder Wusun, invade princess—then issued five generals’ armies hundred fifty thousand riders hunt their south; while Marquis Changluo with Wusun fifty thousand riders shake their west—all reached limits and returned.
translations.idiomatic: When Benshi opened the Xiongnu meant to raid Wusun and seize the princess—Han answered with one hundred fifty thousand riders under five generals in the south and Changluo’s fifty thousand Wusun allies in the west until both wings withdrew at the frontier.

sentences.zh: 時鮮有所獲，徒奮揚威武，明漢兵若雷風耳。

translations.literal: At the time seldom had captures; only roused display martial awe—make clear Han troops like thunder wind ears.
translations.idiomatic: Booty was scarce yet the demonstration proved Han armies strike like thunder.

sentences.zh: 雖空行空反，尚誅兩將軍。

translations.literal: Though empty march empty return, still executed two generals.
translations.idiomatic: Even barren marches cost two generals their heads.

sentences.zh: 故北狄不服，中國未得高枕安寢也。

translations.literal: Therefore northern Di not submitted—Central States not able high-pillow peaceful sleep.
translations.idiomatic: While the northern Di resist, China cannot sleep with a high pillow.

sentences.zh: 逮至元康、神爵之間，大化神明，鴻恩溥洽，而匈奴內亂，五單于爭立，日逐、呼韓邪攜國歸化，扶伏稱臣，然尚羈縻之，計不顓制。

translations.literal: Until between Yuankang and Shenjue, great transformation spirit-bright, vast grace broadly harmonized; yet Xiongnu internal chaos, five Chanyus struggled stand; Rizhu, Huhanye carrying state returned to civilization, prostrate declared subject—yet still bridled them; planning not sole control.
translations.idiomatic: By Yuankang–Shenjue sagely virtue overflowed while five Chanyus tore the realm apart; Rizhu and Huhanye submitted prostrate—yet Han still ruled them loosely rather than micromanaging.

sentences.zh: 自此之後，欲朝者不距，不欲者不強。

translations.literal: From this afterward, those wishing court—not refused; those not wishing—not forced.
translations.idiomatic: Since then whoever wished to attend was welcomed; whoever declined was not compelled.

sentences.zh: 何者？

translations.literal: Why?
translations.idiomatic: Why?

sentences.zh: 外國天性忿鷙，形容魁健，負力怙氣，難化以善，易隸以惡，其強難詘，其和難得。

translations.literal: Foreign lands Heaven-nature wrath fierce; form appearance stalwart strong; rely strength cling qi—hard transform by goodness; easy subdue by evil; their strength hard bend; their harmony hard obtain.
translations.idiomatic: Outlanders are proud brutes—hard to win with kindness, quick to sour—might bows slowly and peace comes dear.

sentences.zh: 故未服之時，勞師遠攻，傾國殫貨，伏屍流血，破堅拔敵，如彼之難也；

translations.literal: Therefore when not yet submitted, weary armies far attack, overturn state exhaust goods, corpses lie bleeding, break hard seize foe—such their difficulty;
translations.idiomatic: Before submission China drained the realm on distant campaigns—blood, treasure, and siegecraft bought each inch;

sentences.zh: 既服之後，尉薦撫循，交接賂遺，威儀俯仰，如此之備也。

translations.literal: After submitted, comfort recommend soothe, exchange connect bribes gifts, awe-rite bow rise—such completeness.
translations.idiomatic: afterward Han soothed them with stipends, gifts, and elaborate ritual.

sentences.zh: 往時嘗屠大宛之城，蹈烏桓之壘，探姑繒之壁，藉蕩姐之場，艾朝鮮之旃，拔兩越之旗，近不過旬月之役，遠不離二時之勞，固已犁其庭，掃其閭，郡縣而置之，雲徹席捲，後無餘災。

translations.literal: Former times once slaughtered Dayuan’s walls, trod Wuhuan’s ramparts, probed Guzeng’s fortifications, tread Dangjie’s fields, mowed Korean banners, uprooted both Yues’ flags—near campaigns lasted mere weeks; far ones stayed within two seasons—firmly plowing their courts, sweeping their lanes, converting them into commanderies and counties like clouds clearing or mats rolled up, leaving no lingering peril.
translations.idiomatic: Han stormed Dayuan, trampled Wuhuan camps, probed Guzeng’s walls, overran Dangjie’s pastures, cut down Korean standards, and tore down both Yues’ banners—sometimes in weeks, never more than two seasons—leveling courts, sweeping villages, annexing them as commanderies until nothing smoldered behind.

sentences.zh: 唯北狄為不然，真中國之堅敵也。

translations.literal: Only northern Di are not thus—truly Central States’ hard enemy.
translations.idiomatic: Only the northern Di defy that pattern—they remain China’s stubborn foe.

sentences.zh: 三垂比之懸矣，前世重之慈甚，未易可輕也。

translations.literal: Three frontiers compared to it hang far; former ages weighted it very heavily—not easy to treat lightly.
translations.idiomatic: The other three marches differ like sky from earth; past courts honored the north accordingly—it must not be slighted.

sentences.zh: 今單于歸義，懷款誠之心，欲離其庭，陳見於前，此乃上世之遺策，神靈之所想望，國家雖費，不得已者也。

translations.literal: Now the Chanyu returns to righteousness, harbors sincere heart of allegiance, wishes leave his court, array audience before—this is former ages’ leftover strategy, spirit powers’ object of longing; state though costly—cannot avoid.
translations.idiomatic: Today the Chanyu offers good faith, leaves his headquarters, and kneels before the throne—a blessing ancestors prayed for; expense is unavoidable.

sentences.zh: 奈何距以來厭之辭，疏以無日之期，消往昔之恩，開將來之隙！

translations.literal: How reject him with coming-aspressing-down wording; estrange with dateless appointment—erase former kindness; open future crack!
translations.idiomatic: How then refuse him with omens of ill luck, stall him without a fixed date, squander past kindness, and invite future hatred?

sentences.zh: 夫款而隙之，使有恨心，負前言，緣往辭，歸怨於漢，因以自絕，終無北面之心，威之不可，諭之不能，焉得不為大憂乎！

translations.literal: As for treating cordially yet estranging him—making him harbor resentment; betraying earlier pledges; clinging to prior rhetoric; shifting blame onto Han; thereby cutting himself off; ultimately losing any wish to face north—neither awe nor persuasion avails—how can this not be grave peril?
translations.idiomatic: Snub a willing ally and you breed bitterness: he recasts old bonds as slights, blames Han, and refuses ever again to bow north—neither intimidation nor reason wins him back. What crisis could loom larger?

sentences.zh: 夫明者視於無形，聰者聽於無聲，誠先於未然，即蒙恬、樊噲不復施，棘門、細柳不復備，馬邑之策安所設，衛、霍之功何得用，五將之威安所震？

translations.literal: The perspicacious sees in formlessness; the acute hears in silence; sincerity precedes not-yet-happening—then Meng Tian, Fan Kuai need not deploy; Jimen, Xiliu need not prepare; Mayi stratagem where apply; Wei, Huo’s achievements how obtain use; five generals’ awe where shake?
translations.idiomatic: The wise spot danger before shape or sound—then Meng Tian and Fan Kuai stay sheathed, Jimen and Xiliu stand down, Mayi plots idle, Wei-Huo feats sleep, and five-generals thunder never rolls.

sentences.zh: 不然，一有隙之後，雖智者勞心於內，辯者轂擊於外，猶不若未然之時也。

translations.literal: If not so—once there is crack afterward, though wise ones toil heart within, eloquent ones hub-strike without—still not like not-yet-happened times.
translations.idiomatic: Otherwise, after the breach even genius within and diplomacy abroad cannot match prevention.

sentences.zh: 且往者圖西域，制車師，置城郭都護三十六國，費歲以大萬計者，豈為康居、烏孫能逾白龍堆而寇西邊哉？

translations.literal: Moreover formerly plotted Western Regions, controlled Cheshi, placed walled cities Protector-General thirty-six states—expense yearly counted in ten thousands—was it because Kangju, Wusun could cross White Dragon Mound and raid western border?
translations.idiomatic: We poured yearly fortunes into thirty-six western states and Cheshi—was that because Kangju or Wusun might cross the White Dragon Desert?

sentences.zh: 乃以制匈奴也。

translations.literal: Thus by means to control Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: It was to pin the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 夫百年勞之，一日失之，費十而愛一，臣竊為國不安也。

translations.literal: For a hundred years labor it; one day lose it—expense ten yet cherish one—your servant privately for state uneasy.
translations.idiomatic: Centuries of labor squandered in an afternoon—throwing away ten for one strikes me as madness for the realm.

sentences.zh: 唯陛下少留意於未亂未戰，以遏邊萌之禍。

translations.literal: Only may Your Majesty slightly attend to before chaos before battle—thereby stop border sprouts’ calamity.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you heed trouble before war erupts and choke off this frontier spark.

sentences.zh: 書奏，天子寤焉，召還匈奴使者，更報單于書而許之。

translations.literal: Memorial submitted; Son of Heaven awakened thereto—recalled Xiongnu envoys, anew replied Chanyu letter and permitted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor awoke to the plea, recalled the Xiongnu embassy, and answered with permission.

sentences.zh: 賜雄帛五十匹，黃金十斤。

translations.literal: Granted Xiong fifty bolts silk, ten jin gold.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xiong received fifty bolts of silk and ten catties of gold.

sentences.zh: 單于未發，會病，復遣使願朝明年。

translations.literal: Chanyu not yet departed—happened ill; again sent envoys wishing court next year.
translations.idiomatic: Before setting out the Chanyu fell ill and asked to postpone his visit until the following year.

sentences.zh: 故事，單于朝，從名王以下及從者二百餘人。

translations.literal: Precedent: when Chanyu courts—from titled kings downward and followers over two hundred persons.
translations.idiomatic: Precedent limited him to titled kings and two hundred retainers.

sentences.zh: 單于又上書言：「蒙天子神靈，人民盛壯，願從五百人入朝，以明天子盛德。

translations.literal: Chanyu again submitted memorial speaking: ‘Receiving Son of Heaven spirit power, people masses flourishing strong—wish follow five hundred persons enter court, thereby clarify Son of Heaven flourishing virtue.’
translations.idiomatic: He memorialized again: ‘Heaven’s favor leaves my people vigorous—I ask leave to bring five hundred followers to display Your Majesty’s greatness.’

sentences.zh: 」上皆許之。

translations.literal: The emperor all permitted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor granted every request.

sentences.zh: 元壽二年，單于來朝，上以太歲厭勝所在，捨之上林苑蒲陶宮。

translations.literal: Yuanshou second year, Chanyu came to court; emperor because Grand Year apotropaion location lodged him at Shanglin Park Grape Palace.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou 2 (1 BCE) he attended audience and was quartered at the Grape Palace in Shanglin because of the Grand Year taboo.

sentences.zh: 告之以加敬於單于，單于知之。

translations.literal: Informed him thereby add respect toward Chanyu—Chanyu knew it.
translations.idiomatic: Officials explained the honor paid him, and he understood.

sentences.zh: 加賜衣三百七十襲，錦繡繒帛三萬匹，絮三萬斤，它如河平時。

translations.literal: Additional grants clothing three hundred seventy suits, brocade figured silk thirty thousand bolts, floss thirty thousand jin; other like Heping times.
translations.idiomatic: He received three hundred seventy changes of robe, thirty thousand bolts of figured silk, thirty thousand catties of wadding, and other gifts on the Heping scale.

sentences.zh: 既罷，遣中郎將韓況送單于。

translations.literal: When finished, sent General of the Gentlemen of the Household Han Kuang escort Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: After the rites Han Kuang, General of the Gentlemen of the Household, escorted him out.

sentences.zh: 單于出塞，到休屯井，北度車田盧水，道裡回遠。

translations.literal: Chanyu exited passes, reached Xiutun Well, north crossed Chetianlu Water—road mileage winding far.
translations.idiomatic: Beyond the wall he reached Xiutun Well, forded the Chetianlu River, and faced a long roundabout march.

sentences.zh: 況等乏食，單于乃給其糧，失期不還五十餘日。

translations.literal: Kuang and others lacked food; Chanyu then supplied their grain—missed deadline not returned more than fifty days.
translations.idiomatic: When Kuang’s escort ran short the Chanyu fed them, delaying their return more than fifty days past schedule.

sentences.zh: 初，上遣稽留昆隨單于去，到國，復遣稽留昆同母兄右大且方與婦入待。

translations.literal: At first, emperor sent Jiliukun follow Chanyu depart; reaching state, again sent Jiliukun same-mother elder brother Right Great Juqu Fang with wife enter attendance.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier the court sent Jiliukun home with the Chanyu, then summoned his half-brother Right Great Juqu Fang and Fang’s wife as hostages.

sentences.zh: 還歸，復遣且方同母兄左日逐王都與婦人侍。

translations.literal: Returned home; again sent Fang’s same-mother elder brother Left Rizhu Wang Du with women attend.
translations.idiomatic: When they returned Han demanded Fang’s other half-brother Du, Left Rizhu king, along with female attendants.

sentences.zh: 是時，漢平帝幼，太皇太后稱制，新都侯王莽秉政，欲說太后以威德至盛異於前，乃風單于令遣王昭君女須卜居次雲入侍太后，所以常賜之甚厚。

translations.literal: At this time Han Emperor Ping young; Grand Empress Dowager held regency; Marquis of Xindu Wang Mang grasped government—wishing persuade Dowager with awe-virtue utmost flourishing different from before—then hinted Chanyu order send Wang Zhaojun’s daughter Xubu princess Yun enter attend Dowager—thereby often grants very generous.
translations.idiomatic: While boy emperor Ping reigned under Grand Empress Dowager Wang Zhengjun, Wang Mang directed affairs and suggested the Chanyu send Lady Yun—Zhaojun’s Xubu daughter—to serve the Dowager, earning lavish gifts.

sentences.zh: 會西域車師後王姑句、去胡來王唐兜皆怨恨都護校尉，將妻子人民亡降匈奴，語在《西域傳》。

translations.literal: It happened Western Regions Rear Cheshi King Guju and King Tangdou of Qu-hu-lai both resented Protector-General Colonel—led wives children people fled surrendered Xiongnu—the words are in ‘Western Regions Traditions.’
translations.idiomatic: Cheshi’s rear king Guju and Qu-hu-lai king Tangdou, bitter at the Protector-General, fled with followers to the Xiongnu—the tale appears in the Traditions of the Western Regions.

sentences.zh: 單于受置左谷蠡地，遣使上書言狀曰：「臣謹已受。

translations.literal: Chanyu received placed them in Left Gu-li territory; sent envoys submitting memorial stating circumstance saying: ‘Your servant respectfully already accepted.’
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu settled them on Left Gu-li pasture and memorialized: ‘Your servant has duly received them.’

sentences.zh: 」詔遣中郎將韓隆、王昌、副校尉甄阜、侍中謁者帛敞、長水校尉王歙使匈奴，告單于曰：「西域內屬，不當得受，今遣之。

translations.literal: Edict sent Generals of Gentlemen Han Long, Wang Chang; Deputy Colonels Zhen Fu; Palace Attendant Herald Bo Chang; Changshui Colonel Wang Xi as envoys to Xiongnu—inform Chanyu saying: ‘Western Regions inner subjects—not fitting ought receive—now send them back.’
translations.idiomatic: An edict dispatched Han Long, Wang Chang, Zhen Fu, Bo Chang, and Wang Xi to tell him: ‘The Western Regions belong to Han—you cannot shelter fugitives; return them.’

sentences.zh: 」單于曰：「孝宣、孝元皇帝哀憐，為作約束，自長城以南天子有之，長城以北單于有之。

translations.literal: Chanyu said: ‘Emperors Xiaoxuan and Xiaoyuan pitied—made covenants: from Great Wall south Son of Heaven possesses; Great Wall north Chanyu possesses.’
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu answered: ‘Emperors Xuan and Yuan drew the compact—south of the Wall belongs to the Son of Heaven, north to us.’

sentences.zh: 有犯塞，輒以狀聞；

translations.literal: If violate passes—then report circumstances;
translations.idiomatic: Border violations must be reported;

sentences.zh: 有降者，不得受。

translations.literal: If there are surrenderers—must not accept.
translations.idiomatic: neither side may harbor defectors from the other.’

sentences.zh: 臣知父呼韓邪單于蒙無量之恩，死遺言曰：『有從中國來降者，勿受，輒送至塞，以報天子厚恩。

translations.literal: Your servant knows father Chanyu Huhanye received measureless grace; dying left testament saying: ‘If there are from Central States come surrenderers—do not accept—then escort to passes—to repay Son of Heaven thick grace.’
translations.idiomatic: ‘My father Huhanye owed Han boundless kindness and charged his heirs: “Reject Han defectors and march them to the frontier—that repays the emperor.”’

sentences.zh: 』此外國也，得受之。

translations.literal: These are outer states—may receive them.’
translations.idiomatic: But those peoples lie beyond Han—we may shelter them.’

sentences.zh: 」使者曰：「匈奴骨肉相攻，國幾絕，蒙中國大恩，危亡復續，妻子完安，累世相繼，宜有以報厚恩。

translations.literal: Envoys said: ‘Xiongnu flesh-bones mutually attacked—state nearly cut off; received Central States great grace—peril extinction again continued; wives children intact safe—piled generations succeeding—fitting have means repay thick grace.’
translations.idiomatic: The envoys retorted: ‘Your kin tore the realm apart until Han restored you—wives and heirs survive thanks to us—you owe more than clever parsing.’

sentences.zh: 」單于叩頭謝罪，執二虜還付使者。

translations.literal: Chanyu knocked head apologized guilt—seized two captives returned handed envoys.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu kowtowed in apology and handed both fugitives to Han.

sentences.zh: 詔使中郎將王萌待西域惡都奴界上逆受。

translations.literal: Edict ordered General of Gentlemen Wang Meng wait at Western Regions Edu-nu border to receive them.
translations.idiomatic: The court told Wang Meng to meet them at the Edu-nu frontier.

sentences.zh: 單于遣使送到國，因請其罪。

translations.literal: Chanyu sent envoys escort them into Han territory—thereby begging forgiveness for his offense.
translations.idiomatic: He escorted them to the border and pleaded mitigation.

sentences.zh: 使者以聞，有詔不聽，會西域諸國王斬以示之。

translations.literal: Envoys thereby reported—edict refused mercy—meanwhile Western Regions kings executed them as demonstration.
translations.idiomatic: Han refused clemency and executed the kings before Western Regions rulers as warning.

sentences.zh: 乃造設四條：中國人亡入匈奴者，烏孫亡降匈奴者，西域諸國佩中國印綬降匈奴者，烏桓降匈奴者，皆不得受。

translations.literal: Then fashioned establish four articles: Central States people fleeing into Xiongnu; Wusun fleeing surrendering Xiongnu; Western Regions states wearing Han seals surrendering Xiongnu; Wuhuan surrendering Xiongnu—all must not accept.
translations.idiomatic: They drafted four clauses barring reception of Han fugitives, Wusun defectors, western kings bearing Han seals, or Wuhuan who fled north.

sentences.zh: 遣中郎將王駿、王昌、副校尉甄阜、王尋使匈奴，班四條與單于，雜函封，付單于，令奉行，因收故宣帝所為約束封函還。

translations.literal: Sent Generals Wang Jun, Wang Chang; Deputy Colonels Zhen Fu, Wang Xun as envoys to Xiongnu—promulgate four articles to Chanyu, mixed-letter sealed, hand Chanyu, order obey execute—thereby collect former Emperor Xuan’s made covenant sealed letters return.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Jun, Wang Chang, Zhen Fu, and Wang Xun delivered the sealed four articles, ordered compliance, and reclaimed Emperor Xuan’s old treaty packets.

sentences.zh: 時，莽奏令中國不得有二名，因使使者以風單於，宜上書慕化，為一名，漢必加厚賞。

translations.literal: At the time Mang memorialized ordering Central States not obtain two names—thereby ordered envoys hint Chanyu—fitting submit memorial admire transformation, make one name—Han surely add thick rewards.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang had forbidden double personal names in China and hinted through envoys that the Chanyu should memorialize sinicization under a single name for richer gifts.

sentences.zh: 單于從之，上書言：「幸得備籓臣，竅樂太平聖制，臣故名囊知牙斯，今謹更名曰知。

translations.literal: Chanyu followed it, submitting memorial speaking: ‘Fortunately obtain prepare screen minister—keenly rejoice Great Peace sage institutions—your servant former name Nangzhiyasi; now respectfully change name called Zhi.’
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu obliged: ‘Honored to serve as vassal, I rejoice in Han civilization; my old name Nangzhiyasi I hereby shorten to Zhi.’

sentences.zh: 」莽大說，白太后，遣使者答諭，厚賞賜焉。

translations.literal: Mang greatly pleased—reported Dowager—sent envoys answer instruct—thick rewards granted him.
translations.idiomatic: Delighted, Mang told the Dowager, answered with envoys, and heaped rewards on him.

sentences.zh: 漢既班四條，後護烏桓使者告烏桓民，毋得復與匈奴皮布稅。

translations.literal: Han having promulgated four articles—later Protector Wuhuan envoy told Wuhuan people—must not again with Xiongnu fur-cloth tax.
translations.idiomatic: After publishing the four clauses Han’s Wuhuan commissioner forbade further fur-and-cloth tribute to the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 匈奴以故事遣使者責烏桓稅，匈奴人民婦女欲賈販者皆隨往焉。

translations.literal: Xiongnu by precedent sent envoys demand Wuhuan tax—Xiongnu people women wishing trade peddle all followed going.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu sent collectors under old custom, followed by traders and women hawkers.

sentences.zh: 烏桓距曰：「奉天子詔條，不當予匈奴稅。

translations.literal: Wuhuan refused saying: ‘Upholding Son of Heaven edict articles—not fitting give Xiongnu tax.’
translations.idiomatic: Wuhuan leaders refused: ‘Han edicts forbid paying your levy.’

sentences.zh: 」匈奴使怒，收烏桓酋豪，縛到懸之。

translations.literal: Xiongnu envoy angry—seized Wuhuan chiefs—bound arrived hanged them.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu envoy seized Wuhuan headmen, bound them, and left them hanging.

sentences.zh: 酋豪昆弟怒，共殺匈奴使及其官屬，收略婦女馬牛。

translations.literal: Chiefs’ elder-younger brothers angry—together killed Xiongnu envoy and his officials—seized plundered women horses cattle.
translations.idiomatic: The headmen’s kinsmen slew the envoy and his escort and drove off women, horses, and cattle.

sentences.zh: 單于聞之，遣使發左賢王兵入烏桓責殺使者，因攻擊之。

translations.literal: Chanyu hearing it—sent envoys mobilize Left Virtuous King troops enter Wuhuan reprove killing envoy—thereupon attacked them.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu mobilized the Left Virtuous king’s men against Wuhuan to avenge the murder, then struck.

sentences.zh: 烏桓分散，或走上山，或東保塞。

translations.literal: Wuhuan scattered—some fled up mountains—some east guarded passes.
translations.idiomatic: Wuhuan bands fled to the hills or east toward the Han wall.

sentences.zh: 匈奴頗殺人民，驅婦女弱小且千人去，置左地，告烏桓曰：「持馬畜皮布來贖之。

translations.literal: Xiongnu greatly killed people—drove women weak small nearly thousand away—placed in left territory—told Wuhuan saying: ‘Bring horses livestock furs cloth come ransom them.’
translations.idiomatic: The raiders killed many, herded nearly a thousand women and children to the eastern pastures, and demanded horses, stock, pelts, and cloth for ransom.’

sentences.zh: 」烏桓見略者親屬二千餘人持財畜往贖，匈奴受，留不遣。

translations.literal: Wuhuan kin of plundered ones over two thousand bearing wealth livestock went ransom—Xiongnu accepted—detained not release.
translations.idiomatic: More than two thousand relatives arrived with ransom goods; the Xiongnu pocketed it but kept the captives.

sentences.zh: 王莽之篡位也，建國元年，遣五威將王駿率甄阜、王颯、陳饒、帛敞、丁業六人，多繼金帛，重遺單于，諭曉以受命代漢狀，因易單于故印。

translations.literal: When Wang Mang usurped—Jianguo first year—sent Five Might general Wang Jun leading Zhen Fu, Wang Sa, Chen Rao, Bo Chang, Ding Ye six men—much carrying gold silk—heavy gifts Chanyu—instructed dawn him receiving mandate replacing Han circumstances—thereby exchange Chanyu old seal.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang seized the throne in Jianguo 1 (9 CE) he sent Wang Jun’s Five Might mission with Zhen Fu, Wang Sa, Chen Rao, Bo Chang, and Ding Ye—laden with gold and silk—to announce Xin’s succession and swap the Chanyu’s old seal.

sentences.zh: 故印文曰「匈奴單於璽」，莽更曰「新匈奴單于章」。

translations.literal: Old seal text said ‘Xiongnu Chanyu xi’—Mang changed to say ‘New Xiongnu Chanyu zhang.’
translations.idiomatic: The old inscription read ‘Seal of the Xiongnu Chanyu’; Mang replaced it with ‘Seal of the Xiongnu Chanyu of Xin.’

sentences.zh: 將率既至，授單于印紱，詔令上故印拔。

translations.literal: Generals arrived—bestowed Chanyu seal-cord—edict ordered submit old seal strip.
translations.idiomatic: The generals presented the new ribbon-seal and ordered surrender of the old.

sentences.zh: 單于再拜受詔。

translations.literal: Chanyu twice bowed received edict.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu bowed twice and accepted the decree.

sentences.zh: 譯前，欲解取故印紱，單于舉掖授之。

translations.literal: Translator stepped forward—wished untie take old seal-cord—Chanyu raised sleeve handed it.
translations.idiomatic: As the interpreter moved to unfasten the old seal, the Chanyu lifted his sleeve to hand it over.

sentences.zh: 左姑夕侯蘇從旁謂單于曰：「未見新印文，宜且勿與。

translations.literal: Left Gu-xi Hou Su from side told Chanyu saying: ‘Not yet seen new seal text—fitting temporarily not give.’
translations.idiomatic: Su of Left Gu-xi whispered: ‘Do not surrender it until we read the new inscription.’

sentences.zh: 」單于止，不肯與。

translations.literal: Chanyu stopped—unwilling give.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu paused and withheld it.

sentences.zh: 請使者坐穹廬，單于欲前為壽。

translations.literal: Requested envoys sit yurt—Chanyu wished step forward make toast.
translations.idiomatic: He invited the envoys into his yurt and stepped up to offer a toast.

sentences.zh: 五威將曰：「故印紱當以時上。

translations.literal: Five Might general said: ‘Old seal-cord ought timely submit.’
translations.idiomatic: The Five Might general insisted: ‘You must surrender the old seal now.’

sentences.zh: 」單于曰：「諾。

translations.literal: Chanyu said: ‘Yes.’
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu answered: ‘Very well.’

sentences.zh: 」復舉掖授譯。

translations.literal: Again raised sleeve handed translator.
translations.idiomatic: Again he raised his sleeve toward the interpreter.

sentences.zh: 蘇復曰：「未見印文，且勿與。

translations.literal: Su again said: ‘Not yet seen seal text—temporarily not give.’
translations.idiomatic: Su repeated: ‘Still no sight of the wording—hold it back.’

sentences.zh: 」單于曰：「印文何由變更！

translations.literal: Chanyu said: ‘Seal text how cause change!’
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu snapped: ‘How could the inscription change!’

sentences.zh: 」遂解故印紱奉上，將率受。

translations.literal: Thereupon untied old seal-cord offered up—generals received.
translations.idiomatic: He removed the old seal and handed it over; the generals took it.

sentences.zh: 著新紱，不解視印，飲食至夜乃罷。

translations.literal: Put on new cord—not untied viewed seal—drank ate until night then stopped.
translations.idiomatic: They fixed the new ribbon without inspecting the chop and feasted until nightfall.

sentences.zh: 右率陳饒謂諸將率曰：「鄉者姑夕侯疑印文，幾令單于不與人。

translations.literal: Right leader Chen Rao told various generals saying: ‘Just now Gu-xi Hou doubted seal text—nearly ordered Chanyu not give people.’
translations.idiomatic: Chen Rao, right-column leader, warned: ‘Gu-xi’s doubts nearly kept the Chanyu from yielding it.’

sentences.zh: 如令視印，見其變改，必求故印，此非辭說所能距也。

translations.literal: If order view seal—see its alteration—surely seek old seal—this not rhetoric able refuse.
translations.idiomatic: If he reads the new legend he will demand the old back—eloquence cannot refuse him.

sentences.zh: 既得而復失之，辱命莫大焉。

translations.literal: Already obtained yet again lost it—disgrace mandate none greater.
translations.idiomatic: To win it then lose it would shame the mission beyond measure.

sentences.zh: 不如椎破故印，以絕禍根。

translations.literal: Better mallet smash old seal—thereby cut off calamity root.
translations.idiomatic: Smash the old seal now and choke off future trouble.’

sentences.zh: 」將率猶與，莫有應者。

translations.literal: Generals hesitated wavered—none had responder.
translations.idiomatic: The commanders wavered and none answered.

sentences.zh: 饒，燕士，果悍，即引斧椎壞之。

translations.literal: Rao—Yan knight—indeed fierce—then drew axe mallet smashed it.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Rao, a grim Yan man, seized an axe and shattered it.

sentences.zh: 明日，單于果遣右骨都侯當白將率曰：「漢賜單于印，言『璽』，不言『章』，又無『漢』字。

translations.literal: Next day—Chanyu indeed sent Right Gutuhou Dangbai tell generals saying: ‘Han grants Chanyu seal—says xi—not saying zhang—also lacks Han character.’
translations.idiomatic: Next morning the Right Gutuhou announced: ‘Han’s gift reads xi, not zhang, and bears no Han legend.’

sentences.zh: 諸王已下乃有『漢』，言『章』。

translations.literal: Various kings downward then have ‘Han’—say ‘zhang.’
translations.idiomatic: Lesser kings bear the Han legend and the word zhang.’

sentences.zh: 今即去『璽』加『新』，與臣下無別。

translations.literal: Now immediately remove ‘xi’ add ‘Xin’—with ministers underlings no distinction.
translations.idiomatic: Now xi is stripped for Xin—I rank no higher than a minister.’

sentences.zh: 願得故印。

translations.literal: Wish obtain old seal.
translations.idiomatic: I beg for my old seal.’

sentences.zh: 」將率示以故印，謂曰：「新室順天製作，故印隨將率所自為破壞。

translations.literal: Generals showed him old seal—told saying: ‘New House obeys Heaven making—old seal following generals themselves smashed.’
translations.idiomatic: They displayed the fragments: ‘Xin follows Heaven’s mandate—the old seal broke under our hands.’

sentences.zh: 單于宜承天命，奉新室之制。

translations.literal: Chanyu ought receive Heaven’s mandate—uphold New House’s institutions.
translations.idiomatic: Accept Heaven’s new order and Xin’s institutions.’

sentences.zh: 」當還白，單于知已無可奈何，又多得賂遺，即遣弟右賢王輿奉馬牛隨將率入謝，因上書求故印。

translations.literal: Dang returned reported—Chanyu knew already nothing to be done—again obtained many bribes gifts—then sent younger brother Right Virtuous King Yu offering horses cattle follow generals enter apology—thereby submitted memorial seek old seal.
translations.idiomatic: Dang’s report left the Chanyu helpless despite heavy gifts; he sent his brother Yu with herds to apologize at court and memorialized again for the old seal.

sentences.zh: 將率還到左犁汗王鹹所居地，見烏桓民多，以問鹹。

translations.literal: Generals returned arrived Left Lihan Wang Xian’s dwelling land—saw Wuhuan people many—thereby asked Xian.
translations.idiomatic: Marching through Left Lihan king Xian’s turf they noticed crowds of Wuhuan and questioned him.

sentences.zh: 鹹具言狀，將率曰：「前封四條，不得受烏桓降者，亟還之。

translations.literal: Xian detailed stated circumstances—generals said: ‘Former sealed four articles—not permitted receive Wuhuan surrenderers—urgently return them.’
translations.idiomatic: Xian explained; the generals ordered: ‘The four clauses forbid sheltering Wuhuan fugitives—send them back at once.’

sentences.zh: 」咸陽：「請密與單于相聞，得語，歸之。

translations.literal: Xian said: ‘Request secretly communicate with Chanyu—obtain words—return them.’
translations.idiomatic: Xian replied: ‘Let me inform the Chanyu secretly first and release them once he answers.’

sentences.zh: 」單于使鹹報曰：「當從塞內還之邪，從塞外還之邪？

translations.literal: Chanyu ordered Xian reply saying: ‘Ought from within passes return them—or from outside passes return them?’
translations.idiomatic: Through Xian the Chanyu asked whether repatriation should run inside or outside the wall.’

sentences.zh: 」將率不敢顓決，以聞。

translations.literal: Generals dared not sole decide—thereby reported.
translations.idiomatic: The generals refused to decide alone and memorialized.

sentences.zh: 詔報，從塞外還之。

translations.literal: Edict replied—from outside passes return them.
translations.idiomatic: The reply ordered release beyond the frontier.

sentences.zh: 單于始用夏侯籓求地有距漢語，後以求稅烏桓不得，因寇略其人民，釁由是生，重以印文改易，故怨恨。

translations.literal: Chanyu began employ Xiahou Fan seeking land having rejecting Han words—afterward by seeking tax Wuhuan not obtaining—thereupon raided plundered their people—rift thereby arose—doubled by seal text alteration—therefore resentment hatred.
translations.idiomatic: Resentment began when Fan’s land grab insulted him, deepened when Wuhuan tribute failed and raids followed, and peaked when the seal legend changed.

sentences.zh: 乃遣右大且渠蒲呼盧訾等十餘人將兵眾萬騎，以護送烏桓為名，勒兵朔方塞下。

translations.literal: Then sent Right Great Juqu Puhuluzi etc. over ten persons leading troops multitudes ten thousand riders—using escort Wuhuan as name—marshal troops Shuofang below passes.
translations.idiomatic: He dispatched Puhuluzi and a dozen nobles with ten thousand riders under pretense of escorting Wuhuan, camping them beneath Shuofang.

sentences.zh: 朔方太守以聞。

translations.literal: Shuofang governor thereby reported.
translations.idiomatic: The Shuofang governor reported it upward.

sentences.zh: 明年，西域車師後王須置離謀降匈奴，都護但欽誅斬之。

translations.literal: Next year—Western Regions Rear Cheshi King Xuzhili plotted surrender Xiongnu—Protector-General Dan Qin executed beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Rear Cheshi king Xuzhili plotted defection; Protector-General Dan Qin executed him.

sentences.zh: 置離兄狐蘭支將人眾二千餘人，驅畜產，舉國亡降匈奴，單于受之。

translations.literal: Xuzhili’s elder brother Hulanzhi led people multitudes over two thousand—drove livestock—whole state fled surrendered Xiongnu—Chanyu accepted them.
translations.idiomatic: His brother Hulanzhi led two thousand followers with herds into exile; the Chanyu admitted them.

sentences.zh: 狐蘭支與匈奴共入寇，擊車師，殺後成長，傷都護司馬，復還入匈奴。

translations.literal: Hulanzhi together with Xiongnu jointly entered raid—struck Cheshi—killed rear chief—wounded Protector-General Major—again returned entered Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: With Xiongnu allies he struck Cheshi, slew its chief, wounded the Protector-General’s major, and withdrew north.

sentences.zh: 時，戊己校尉史陳良、終帶、司馬丞韓玄、右曲候任商等見西域頗背叛，聞匈奴欲大侵，恐並死，即謀劫略吏卒數百人，共殺戊己校尉刀護，遣人與匈奴南犁汗王南將軍相聞。

translations.literal: At the time—Colonel of Wu-ji clerks Chen Liang, Zhong Dai—Major Assistant Han Xuan—Right Quhou Ren Shang etc. seeing Western Regions greatly rebel—heard Xiongnu wished great invasion—feared together die—then plotted seize plunder officials soldiers several hundred persons—together killed Colonel of Wu-ji Dao Hu—sent persons connect with Xiongnu South Lihan king South General.
translations.idiomatic: Wu-ji clerks Chen Liang and Zhong Dai, aide Han Xuan, and Ren Shang of the Right Qu seeing the west unravel and fearing a massive raid, mutinied with hundreds of troops, slew Colonel Dao Hu, and contacted the Xiongnu South General.

sentences.zh: 匈奴南將軍二千騎入西域迎良等，良等盡脅略戊己校尉吏士男女二千餘人入匈奴。

translations.literal: Xiongnu South General two thousand riders entered Western Regions welcome Liang etc.—Liang etc. all coerced plundered Wu-ji Colonel officials soldiers men women over two thousand entered Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: The South General sent two thousand riders to meet them; Liang dragged more than two thousand Wu-ji troops and families into the steppe.

sentences.zh: 玄、商留南將軍所，良、帶徑至單于庭，人眾別置零吾水上田居。

translations.literal: Xuan, Shang remained South General’s place—Liang, Dai went straight to Chanyu court—people multitudes separately placed Lingwu River above field dwell.
translations.idiomatic: Han and Ren stayed with the South General while Liang and Zhong reached headquarters; dependents were settled to farm along the Lingwu River.

sentences.zh: 單于號良、帶曰烏桓都將軍，留居單于所，數呼與飲食。

translations.literal: Chanyu titled Liang, Dai called Wuhuan Capital General—detained dwell Chanyu’s place—repeatedly summoned with drink food.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu styled them Wuhuan supreme generals, kept them at court, and often feasted them.

sentences.zh: 西域都護但欽上書言匈奴南將軍右伊秩訾將人眾冠擊諸國。

translations.literal: Western Regions Protector-General Dan Qin submitted memorial spoke Xiongnu South General Right Yizhi leading people multitudes raid strike various states.
translations.idiomatic: Dan Qin reported the South General and Right Yizhi leading raids across the western states.

sentences.zh: 莽於是大分匈奴為十五單于，遣中郎將藺苞、副校尉戴級將兵萬騎，多繼珍寶至雲中塞下，招誘呼韓邪單于諸子，欲以次拜之。

translations.literal: Mang thereupon greatly divided Xiongnu into fifteen Chanyus—sent General of Gentlemen Lin Bao, Deputy Colonel Dai Ji leading troops ten thousand riders—much carrying treasures arrived Yunzhong below passes—summon lure Huhanye Chanyu various sons—wishing by order invest them.
translations.idiomatic: Mang carved the Xiongnu into fifteen rival Chanyus and sent Lin Bao and Dai Ji with ten thousand riders and treasures to Yunzhong to lure Huhanye’s sons for sequential investiture.

sentences.zh: 使譯出塞誘呼右犁汗王鹹、鹹子登、助三人，至則脅拜鹹為孝單于，賜安車鼓車各一，黃金千手，雜繒千匹，戲戟十；

translations.literal: Ordered translator exit passes lure summon Right Lihan Wang Xian—Xian’s sons Deng, Zhu three persons—arriving then coerced invested Xian as Filial Chanyu—granted riding carriage drum carriage each one—gold thousand jin, mixed silk thousand bolts, ceremonial halberds ten;
translations.idiomatic: Interpreters lured Xian and his sons Deng and Zhu beyond the wall, forced Xian to accept the title Filial Chanyu, and gave carriages, a thousand jin of gold, a thousand bolts of silk, and ten halberds;

sentences.zh: 拜助為順單于，賜黃金五百斤；

translations.literal: Invested Zhu as Compliant Chanyu—granted gold five hundred jin;
translations.idiomatic: named Zhu Compliant Chanyu with five hundred jin of gold;

sentences.zh: 傳送助、登長安。

translations.literal: Escorted conveyed Zhu, Deng Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu and Deng were convoyed to Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 莽封苞為宣威公，拜為虎牙將軍；

translations.literal: Mang enfeoffed Bao as Duke of Xuanwei—invested as Tiger-Fang general;
translations.idiomatic: Mang made Bao duke of Xuanwei and Tiger-Fang general;

sentences.zh: 封級為揚威公，拜為虎賁將軍。

translations.literal: Enfeoffed Ji as Duke of Yangwei—invested as Tiger-Rush general.
translations.idiomatic: Ji became duke of Yangwei and Tiger-Rush general.

sentences.zh: 單于聞之，怒曰：「先單于受漢宣帝恩，不可負他。

translations.literal: Chanyu hearing it—angry said: ‘Former Chanyu received Han Emperor Xuandi grace—cannot betray him.’
translations.idiomatic: The true Chanyu raged: ‘My predecessor owed Emperor Xuan of Han—we cannot betray that debt.’

sentences.zh: 今天子非宣帝子孫，何以得立？

translations.literal: Today Son of Heaven not Xuandi descendants—how thereby obtain establishment?
translations.idiomatic: Today’s ruler is no descendant of Xuan—by what right does he reign?’

sentences.zh: 」遣左骨都侯、右伊秩訾王呼盧訾及左賢王樂將兵入雲中益壽塞，大殺吏民。

translations.literal: Sent Left Gutuhou, Right Yizhi Wang Huluzi and Left Virtuous King Le leading troops enter Yunzhong Yishou Pass—greatly killed officials people.
translations.idiomatic: He sent Huluzi and Left Virtuous king Le through Yishou Pass in Yunzhong on a slaughter of officials and townsfolk.

sentences.zh: 是歲，建國三年也。

translations.literal: This year—Jianguo third year.
translations.idiomatic: The year was Jianguo 3 (11 CE).

sentences.zh: 是後，單于歷告左右部都尉、諸邊王，入塞寇盜，大輩萬餘，中輩數千，少者數百，殺雁門、朔方太守、都尉，略吏民畜產不可勝數，緣邊虛耗。

translations.literal: After this—the Chanyu repeatedly notified Left and Right department commandants and various border kings—entered passes raiding stealing—great bands over ten thousand—middle bands several thousand—small ones several hundred—killed Yanmen and Shuofang governors and commandants—plundered officials people livestock beyond counting—along border emptied exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu ordered border commanders and kings to raid the frontier in bands from hundreds to tens of thousands, killing Yanmen and Shuofang officials and stripping the marches bare.

sentences.zh: 莽新即位，怙府庫之富欲立威，乃拜十二部將率，發郡國勇士，武庫精兵，各有所屯守，轉委輸於邊。

translations.literal: Mang newly ascended—relied treasury granaries’ wealth wishing establish awe—then invested twelve department generals—issued commandery-state braves—arsenal elite troops—each had garrison guard—rotated transport conveyance to border.
translations.idiomatic: Freshly enthroned, Wang Mang trusted his treasury and named twelve army columns, drafting local stalwarts and armory troops into frontier posts with endless supply trains.

sentences.zh: 議滿三十萬眾，賁三百日糧，同時十道並出，窮追匈奴，內之於丁令，因分其地，立呼韓邪十五子。

translations.literal: Deliberated fill three hundred thousand masses—prepare three hundred days grain—same time ten routes together exit—exhaust pursue Xiongnu—drive them among Dingling—thereby divide their land—establish Huhanye fifteen sons.
translations.idiomatic: The plan called for three hundred thousand men with three hundred days’ rations on ten simultaneous thrusts to chase the Xiongnu into the Dingling pasture, carve their realm, and enthrone fifteen of Huhanye’s sons.

sentences.zh: 莽將嚴尤諫曰：

translations.literal: Mang’s general Yan You remonstrated saying:
translations.idiomatic: General Yan You objected:

sentences.zh: 臣聞匈奴為害，所從來久矣，未聞上世有必征之者也。

translations.literal: Your servant hears Xiongnu have been harm—from where come long ago—not heard High Ages had must campaign them.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu have plagued China for ages, yet antiquity never insisted on crushing them outright.

sentences.zh: 後世三家周、秦、漢征之，然皆未有得上策者也。

translations.literal: Later ages three houses Zhou, Qin, Han campaigned them—yet all never obtained upper-strategy ones.
translations.idiomatic: Later Zhou, Qin, and Han all marched north—none grasped the best strategy.

sentences.zh: 周得中策，漢得下策，秦無策焉。

translations.literal: Zhou obtained middle strategy—Han obtained lower strategy—Qin had no strategy.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou chose the middling course, Han the lesser, Qin none at all.

sentences.zh: 當周宣王時，獫允內侵，至於涇陽，命將征之，盡境而還。

translations.literal: When King Xuan of Zhou—Xianyun inner invaded—reached Jingyang—ordered generals campaign them—exhausted border then returned.
translations.idiomatic: King Xuan drove the Xianyun from Jingyang with a pursuit that stopped at the frontier.

sentences.zh: 其視戎狄之侵，譬猶蚊虻之螫，驅之而已。

translations.literal: Their viewing Rong-Di invasion—liken like mosquito gadfly sting—drive them only.
translations.idiomatic: They treated barbarian raids like mosquito bites—brush them off.

sentences.zh: 故天下稱明，是為中策。

translations.literal: Therefore All-under-Heaven called enlightened—this is middle strategy.
translations.idiomatic: The realm praised such restraint—the middle path.

sentences.zh: 漢武帝選將練兵，約賁輕糧，深入遠戍，雖有克獲之功，胡輒報之，兵連禍結三十餘年，中國罷耗，匈奴亦創艾，而天下稱武，是為下策。

translations.literal: Emperor Wu of Han selected generals drilled troops—contract carried light grain—deep entered far garrison—though had conquer capture merit—Hu always repaid them—arms linked calamity knotted thirty odd years—Central States weary exhausted—Xiongnu also wounded—yet All-under-Heaven called martial—this is lower strategy.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu picked crack troops, marched light and deep, traded blows for thirty years until both realms bled—history calls it valor but it was the inferior strategy.

sentences.zh: 秦始皇不忍小恥而輕民力，築長城之固，延袤萬里，轉輸之行，起於負海，疆境既完，中國內竭，以喪社稷，是為無策。

translations.literal: Qin Shihuang bore not small shame yet slighted people’s strength—built Great Wall’s solidity—stretch ten thousand li—transport conveyance routes—rose from back-sea—border finished complete—Central States inner drained—thereby lose altars—this is no strategy.
translations.idiomatic: The First Emperor could not swallow slight insults and drafted the realm to wall ten thousand li from the coast inward until China collapsed—that was having no strategy.

sentences.zh: 今天下遭陽九之厄，比年饑饉，西北邊猶甚。

translations.literal: Now All-under-Heaven suffers Yang-nine calamity—successive years famine—northwest border especially severe.
translations.idiomatic: The realm labors under Heaven’s ill-starred cycle; famine stalks year after year—worst in the northwest.

sentences.zh: 發三十萬眾，具三百日糧，東援海代，南取江淮，然後乃備。

translations.literal: Issue three hundred thousand masses—prepare three hundred days grain—east draw Hai-Dai—south take Jiang-Huai—then afterward prepare.
translations.idiomatic: Raising three hundred thousand men with three hundred days’ grain means draining Hai-Dai and Jiang-Huai before you even march.

sentences.zh: 計其道裡，一年尚未集合，兵先至者聚居暴露，師老械弊，勢不可用，此一難也。

translations.literal: Calculate its road mileage—one year still not assembled—troops first arrive crowd dwell exposed—army aged weapons worn—power unusable—this first difficulty.
translations.idiomatic: First difficulty: a year may pass before columns unite—early arrivals camp exposed until troops tire and gear fails.

sentences.zh: 邊既空虛，不能奉軍糧，內調郡國，不相及屬，此二難也。

translations.literal: Border already empty void—cannot supply army grain—inner transfer commandery-states—not mutually reach chain—this second difficulty.
translations.idiomatic: Second: the frontier cannot feed them while interior levies cannot link up in time.

sentences.zh: 計一人三百日食，用糒十八斛，非牛力不能勝；

translations.literal: Calculate one person three hundred days food—uses dried rations eighteen hu—not ox strength cannot bear;
translations.idiomatic: Each soldier needs eighteen hu of grain for three hundred days—only oxen can haul it;

sentences.zh: 牛又當自繼食，加二十斛，重矣。

translations.literal: Oxen again ought self continue eat—add twenty hu—heavy.
translations.idiomatic: the beasts need twenty hu themselves—the burden swells.

sentences.zh: 胡地沙鹵，多乏水草，以往事揆之，軍出未滿百日，牛必物故且盡，餘糧尚多，人不能負，此三難也。

translations.literal: Hu lands sand brackish—much lack water grass—by past affairs gauge—army out not full hundred days—oxen surely die and exhausted—leftover grain still much—people cannot bear—this third difficulty.
translations.idiomatic: Third: desert wastes kill draft oxen within a hundred days, leaving grain men cannot carry.

sentences.zh: 胡地秋冬甚寒，春夏甚風，多繼釜鍑薪炭，重不可勝，食糒飲水，以歷四時，師有疾疫之憂，是故前世伐胡，不過百日，非不欲久，勢力不能，此四難也。

translations.literal: Hu lands autumn winter very cold—spring summer very wind—much carry cauldrons caul bundles charcoal—heavy unbearable—eat dried drink water—pass four seasons—army has pestilence worry—therefore former ages campaign Hu—not exceed hundred days—not not wish long—power cannot—this fourth difficulty.
translations.idiomatic: Fourth: bitter seasons demand stoves and fodder for a full year—past campaigns halted at one hundred days because plague and climate forbade longer stays.

sentences.zh: 輜重自隨，則輕銳者少，不得疾行，虜徐遁逃，勢不能及，幸而逢虜，又累輜重，如遇險阻，銜尾相隨，虜要遮前後，危殆不測，此五難也。

translations.literal: Baggage train self follows—then light crack troops few—cannot swift march—enemy slowly flee escape—power cannot catch—luckily encounter enemy—again drag baggage train—if meet peril obstacle—bit-tail mutually follow—enemy ambush front rear—peril unfathomable—this fifth difficulty.
translations.idiomatic: Fifth: baggage slows pursuit—nomads slip away—or if caught, trains clog narrow ground while enemies strike front and rear.

sentences.zh: 大用民力，功不可必立，臣伏憂之。

translations.literal: Great employ people’s strength—merit cannot surely establish—your servant prostrate worries it.
translations.idiomatic: Mobilizing the realm for uncertain gain fills me with dread.

sentences.zh: 今既發兵，宜縱先至者，令臣尤等深入霆擊，且以創艾胡虜。

translations.literal: Now having issued troops—fitting release first arrivers—order your servant You etc. deep enter thunder strike—temporarily thereby wound Hu captives.
translations.idiomatic: Since troops march, let early arrivals under my command strike deep like lightning and sting the Hu.

sentences.zh: 莽不聽尤言，轉兵谷如故，天下騷動。

translations.literal: Mang did not heed You’s words—rotated troops grain as before—All-under-Heaven stirred restless.
translations.idiomatic: Mang ignored him and kept shifting troops and grain until the empire seethed.

sentences.zh: 鹹既受莽孝單于之號，馳出塞歸庭，具以見脅狀白單于。

translations.literal: Xian having received Mang’s Filial Chanyu title—galloped exit passes return court—fully reported seen coercion circumstances inform Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Xian fled home with Mang’s bogus Filial title and told the true Chanyu how he had been forced.

sentences.zh: 單于更以為於粟置支侯，匈侯賤官也。

translations.literal: Chanyu again made him Yusu Zhizhi marquis—Xiongnu marquis low office.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu instead named him marquis of Yusu Zhizhi—a petty steppe title.

sentences.zh: 後助病死，莽以登代助為順單于。

translations.literal: Afterward Zhu sick died—Mang used Deng replace Zhu as Compliant Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhu died Mang named Deng Compliant Chanyu in his stead.

sentences.zh: 厭難將軍陳欽、震狄將軍王巡屯雲中葛邪塞。

translations.literal: Hardship-quelling General Chen Qin, Barbarian-quaking General Wang Xun encamped Yunzhong Geye Pass.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Qin the Hardship-quelling general and Wang Xun the Barbarian-quaking general camped at Geye Pass in Yunzhong.

sentences.zh: 是時，匈奴數為邊寇，殺將率吏士，略人民，驅畜產去甚眾。

translations.literal: At this time—Xiongnu repeatedly became border raiders—killed generals officials soldiers—plundered people—drove livestock away very many.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu raided ceaselessly, killing commanders and troops and carrying off people and herds.

sentences.zh: 捕得虜生口驗問，皆曰孝單于鹹子角數為寇。

translations.literal: Captured obtained barbarian captives interrogated—all said Filial Chanyu Xian’s son Jue repeatedly became raider.
translations.idiomatic: Prisoners agreed that Jue, son of the puppet Filial Chanyu Xian, led the raids.

sentences.zh: 兩將以聞。

translations.literal: Two generals thereby reported.
translations.idiomatic: Both generals memorialized the fact.

sentences.zh: 四年，莽會諸蠻夷，斬鹹子登於長安市。

translations.literal: Fourth year—Mang assembled various barbarians—beheaded Xian’s son Deng at Chang’an market.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year Wang Mang convened the tribes and executed Deng in Chang’an marketplace.

sentences.zh: 初，北邊自宣帝以來，數世不見煙火之警，人民熾盛，牛馬布野。

translations.literal: At first—northern border since Emperor Xuandi onward—several generations not seen beacon-fire alarms—people flourishing—cattle horses spread wild.
translations.idiomatic: Since Emperor Xuan the north had known generations without beacon fires—people multiplied and herds carpeted the prairie.

sentences.zh: 及莽撓亂匈奴，與之構難，邊民死亡係獲，又十二部兵久屯而不出，吏士罷弊，數年之間，北邊虛空，野有暴骨矣。

translations.literal: Reaching Mang disturbed Xiongnu—with them constructed strife—border people death captive bound—also twelve department troops long encamped but not exit—officials soldiers weary broken—within several years—northern border empty void—wild has exposed bones.
translations.idiomatic: Mang’s provocations killed or enslaved frontier folk while twelve idle armies exhausted the garrisons—within years the north lay empty, bones whitening in the fields.

sentences.zh: 烏珠留單于立二十一歲，建國五年死。

translations.literal: Wuzhuliu Chanyu established twenty-one years—died Jianguo fifth year.
translations.idiomatic: Chanyu Wuzhuliu reigned twenty-one years and died in Jianguo 5 (13 CE).

sentences.zh: 匈奴用事大臣右骨都侯須卜當，即王昭君女伊墨居次雲之婿也。

translations.literal: Xiongnu managing-affairs great minister Right Gutuhou Xubu Dang—namely Wang Zhaojun daughter Yimo princess Yun’s son-in-law.
translations.idiomatic: Power lay with Right Gutuhou Xubu Dang—husband of Lady Yun, Zhaojun’s Yimo daughter.

sentences.zh: 雲常欲與中國和親，又素與鹹厚善，見鹹前後為莽所拜，故遂越輿而立鹹為烏累若E96F單于。

translations.literal: Yun constantly wished with Central States peace kinship—again plain with Xian thick good—seeing Xian front-and-after by Mang invested—therefore then skipped Yu and established Xian as Wulei Ruodi Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Yun favored peace with Han and was close to Xian; seeing Mang twice elevate him, she passed over Yu and raised Xian as Chanyu Wulei Ruodi.

sentences.zh: 烏累單于鹹立，以弟輿為左谷蠡王。

translations.literal: Wulei Chanyu Xian established—appointed younger brother Yu as Left Gu-li King.
translations.idiomatic: Chanyu Wulei Xian named his brother Yu Left Gu-li king.

sentences.zh: 烏珠留單于子蘇屠胡本為左賢王，以弟屠耆閼氏子盧渾為右賢王。

translations.literal: Wuzhuliu Chanyu’s son Sutu Hu originally was Left Virtuous King—appointed younger-brother Tuqi Chanyu Consort’s son Luhun as Right Virtuous King.
translations.idiomatic: Sutu Hu, son of Wuzhuliu, remained Left Virtuous king while Luhun—born to the Tuqi consort’s junior house—was named Right Virtuous king.

sentences.zh: 烏珠留單于在時，左賢王數死，以為其號不祥，更易命左賢王曰「護於」。

translations.literal: When Wuzhuliu Chanyu alive—Left Virtuous King repeatedly died—thought its title inauspicious—changed replaced ordered Left Virtuous King called ‘Huyu.’
translations.idiomatic: Under Wuzhuliu several Left Virtuous kings died in office, so the title was deemed ill-omened and renamed Huyu.

sentences.zh: 護於之尊最貴，次當為單于，故烏珠留單于授其長子以為護於，欲傳以國。

translations.literal: Huyu’s honor highest noble—next ought become Chanyu—therefore Wuzhuliu Chanyu invested his eldest son as Huyu—wished transmit thereby state.
translations.idiomatic: Huyu ranked highest after the Chanyu, so Wuzhuliu named his eldest heir Huyu to pass him the realm.

sentences.zh: 鹹怨烏珠留單于貶賤己號，不欲傳國，及立，貶護於為左屠耆王。

translations.literal: Xian resented Wuzhuliu Chanyu debasing low his title—not wished transmit state—when established—demoted Huyu as Left Tuqi King.
translations.idiomatic: Xian bitterly resented that title and meant to break the succession; once enthroned he reduced Huyu to Left Tuqi king.

sentences.zh: 雲、當遂勸鹹和親。

translations.literal: Yun, Dang then urged Xian peace kinship.
translations.idiomatic: Yun and Dang pressed him toward heqin with Han.

sentences.zh: 天鳳二年五月，莽復遣歙與五威將王鹹率伏黯、丁業等六人，使送右廚唯姑夕王，因奉歸前所斬侍子登及諸貴人從者喪，皆載以常車。

translations.literal: Tianfeng second year fifth month—Mang again sent Xi together with Five Might general Wang Xian leading Fu An, Ding Ye etc. six persons—mission escort Right Kitchen Only Gu-xi Wang—thereby convey return previously beheaded hostage Deng’s and various nobles followers’ remains—all carried on ordinary carts.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month of Tianfeng 2 (15 CE) Mang sent Wang Xi with Fu An, Ding Ye, and four others to escort the Right Kitchen Only Gu-xi king home and return Deng’s remains and those of his attendants in hearses.

sentences.zh: 至塞下，單于遣雲、當子男大且渠奢等至塞迎。

translations.literal: Reached below passes—Chanyu sent Yun, Dang’s son Great Juqu She etc. to passes welcome.
translations.idiomatic: At the frontier the Chanyu sent Yun and She, Dang’s son, to meet them.

sentences.zh: 鹹等至，多遺單于金珍，因諭說改其號，號匈奴曰「恭奴」，單于曰「善於」，賜印綬。

translations.literal: Xian etc. arrived—much gifted Chanyu gold treasures—thereby instructed persuade change his titles—call Xiongnu ‘Gongnu’—call Chanyu ‘Shanyu’—granted seal-cord.
translations.idiomatic: Xian loaded him with treasure and pressed new titles—‘Gongnu’ for the nation and ‘Shanyu’ for its ruler—along with fresh imperial ribbons.

sentences.zh: 封骨都侯當為後安公，當子男奢為後安侯。

translations.literal: Enfeoffed Gutuhou Dang as Duke of Hou’an—Dang’s son She as Marquis of Hou’an.
translations.idiomatic: Dang became duke of Hou’an and his son She marquis of Hou’an.

sentences.zh: 單于貪莽金幣，故曲聽之，然寇盜如故。

translations.literal: Chanyu greedy Mang’s gold coins—therefore bent heard him—yet raiding stealing as before.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu bent his ear for Mang’s gold but raids continued unchecked.

sentences.zh: 鹹、歙又以陳良等購金付雲、當，令自差與之。

translations.literal: Xian, Xi again used Chen Liang etc. bounty gold pay Yun, Dang—ordered self allocate give them.
translations.idiomatic: Xian and Xi handed Yun and Dang the bounty for Chen Liang’s band to distribute.

sentences.zh: 十二月，還入塞，莽大喜，賜歙錢二百萬，悉封黯等。

translations.literal: Twelfth month—returned entered passes—Mang greatly pleased—granted Xi cash two million—all enfeoffed An etc.
translations.idiomatic: They reentered in the twelfth month; Mang rejoiced, gave Xi two million cash, and ennobled Fu An’s party.

sentences.zh: 單于鹹立五歲，天鳳五年死，弟左賢王輿立，為呼都而屍道皋若鞮單于。

translations.literal: Chanyu Xian established five years—died Tianfeng fifth year—younger brother Left Virtuous King Yu established—became Huduershi Daogao Ruodi Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: After five years Xian died in Tianfeng 5 (18 CE); his brother Yu succeeded as Chanyu Huduershi Daogao Ruodi.

sentences.zh: 匈奴謂孝曰「若鞮自呼韓邪後，與漢親密，見漢謚帝為「孝」，慕之，故皆為「若鞮」。

translations.literal: Xiongnu call filial piety ‘ruodi’—from Huhanye onward—intimate with Han—seeing Han posthumous-title emperor as ‘Xiao’—admired it—therefore all use ‘ruodi.’
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu render ‘filial’ as ruodi: since Huhanye’s age of Han friendship they admired Han posthumous ‘Xiao’ and adopted ruodi in their titles.

sentences.zh: 呼都而屍單于輿既立，貪利賞賜，遣大且渠奢與雲女弟當於居次子醯櫝王俱奉獻至長安。

translations.literal: Huduershi Chanyu Yu having established—greedy profit rewards—sent Great Juqu She together with Yun’s younger sister Dangyu princess’s son Xi-du Wang jointly presenting tribute arrived Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: Yu, greedy for Han gifts, sent She with Lady Yun’s nephew the Xi-du king to Chang’an with tribute.

sentences.zh: 莽遣和親侯歙與奢等俱至制虜塞下，與雲、當會，因以兵迫脅，將至長安。

translations.literal: Mang sent Peace-Kinship Marquis Xi together with She etc. all arrived Zhilu Pass below—with Yun, Dang meet—thereby with troops coerce—escort toward Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: Mang sent Marquis Xi to Zhilu Pass to seize Yun and Dang at sword point and march them to Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 雲、當小男從塞下得脫，歸匈奴。

translations.literal: Yun, Dang’s young boy from below passes obtained escape—returned Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Yun and Dang’s youngest son slipped away below the wall and fled north.

sentences.zh: 當至長安，莽拜為須卜單于，欲出大兵以輔立之。

translations.literal: Dang arrived Chang’an—Mang invested as Xubu Chanyu—wished issue great troops thereby aid establish him.
translations.idiomatic: In Chang’an Mang named Dang Xubu Chanyu and planned a grand expedition to enthrone him.

sentences.zh: 兵調度亦不合，而匈奴愈怒，並入北邊，北邊由是壞敗。

translations.literal: Troop deployments also did not fit—yet Xiongnu increasingly angry—together entered northern border—northern border thereby ruined defeated.
translations.idiomatic: Mobilization misfired while the Xiongnu stormed the north and shattered the frontier.

sentences.zh: 會當病死，莽以其庶女陸逮任妻後安公奢，所以尊寵之甚厚，終為欲出兵立之者。

translations.literal: Happened Dang sick died—Mang gave his secondary daughter Lu Dairen as wife to Hou’an Duke She—thereby honor favor him very thick—ultimately because wished issue troops establish him.
translations.idiomatic: After Dang died Mang married a junior princess to She with lavish honors—all to keep alive his scheme of enthroning him by force.

sentences.zh: 會漢兵誅莽，雲、奢亦死。

translations.literal: Happened Han troops executed Mang—Yun, She also died.
translations.idiomatic: Han rebels slew Mang; Yun and She died too.

sentences.zh: 更始二年冬，漢遺中郎將歸德侯颯、大司馬護軍陳遵使匈奴，授單于漢舊制璽綬，王侯以下印綬，因送雲、當余親屬貴人從者。

translations.literal: Gengshi second year winter—Han dispatched General of Gentlemen Marquis of Guide Sa—Grand Marshal Guardsman Chen Zun as envoys Xiongnu—invest Chanyu Han old-system xi-cord—kings marquises downward seal-cords—thereby escort Yun, Dang remainder kin nobles followers.
translations.idiomatic: In winter of Gengshi 2 (24 CE) Han sent Wang Sa and Chen Zun to restore the old Han seals and ribbons below princely rank and escort Yun’s and Dang’s surviving kin.

sentences.zh: 單于輿驕，謂遵、颯曰：「匈奴本與漢為兄弟，匈奴中亂，孝宣皇帝輔立呼韓邪單于，故稱臣以尊漢。

translations.literal: Chanyu Yu arrogant—told Zun, Sa saying: ‘Xiongnu originally with Han were brothers—Xiongnu middle chaos—Emperor Xiaoxuan aided established Huhanye Chanyu—therefore declared subject thereby honor Han.’
translations.idiomatic: Yu boasted to Chen Zun and Wang Sa: ‘We were brothers with Han until chaos struck; Emperor Xuan enthroned Huhanye, so we bowed to honor Han.’

sentences.zh: 今漢亦大亂，為王莽所篡，匈奴亦出兵擊莽，空其邊境，令天下騷動思漢，莽卒以敗而漢復興，亦我力也，當復尊我！

translations.literal: Now Han also great chaos—by Wang Mang usurped—Xiongnu also issued troops strike Mang—emptied its borderlands—ordered All-under-Heaven stirred miss Han—Mang finally thereby defeated yet Han again rose—also my force—ought again honor me!’
translations.idiomatic: ‘Han fell to Mang; we smashed his frontier and turned the realm toward Liu restoration—that was our doing—you owe us renewed precedence!’

sentences.zh: 」遵與相牚距，單于終持此言。

translations.literal: Zun with mutually resisted—Chanyu ultimately held this speech.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Zun argued back but Yu never dropped the demand.

sentences.zh: 其明年夏，還。

translations.literal: The next year summer—returned.
translations.idiomatic: They returned the following summer.

sentences.zh: 會赤眉入長安，更始敗。

translations.literal: Happened Red Eyebrows entered Chang’an—Gengshi defeated.
translations.idiomatic: The Red Eyebrows took Chang’an and toppled Gengshi.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：《書》戒「蠻夷猾夏」，《詩》稱「戎狄是膺」，《春秋》「有道守在四夷」，久矣，夷狄之為患也！

translations.literal: Praise says: Documents warns ‘barbarians disturb Xia’—Odes praises ‘Rong-Di are struck’—Spring and Autumn ‘having Dao guard lies in four barbarians’—long indeed—Yi-Di’s being calamity!
translations.idiomatic: The appraisal states: The Documents warn that barbarians vex the heartland; the Odes cheer striking Rong and Di; the Spring and Autumn says a virtuous reign keeps guard among the four Yi—the alien peril is ancient.

sentences.zh: 故自漢興，忠言嘉謀之臣曷嘗不運籌策相與爭於廟堂之上乎？

translations.literal: Therefore since Han arose—loyal words fine planning ministers how ever not wield stratagems mutually contend upon ancestral temple hall above?
translations.idiomatic: Since Han rose, which worthy minister has not debated strategy in court?

sentences.zh: 高祖時則劉敬，呂後時樊噲、季布，孝文時賈誼、朝錯，李武時王恢、韓安國、硃買臣、公孫弘、董仲舒，人持所見，各有同異，然總其要，歸兩科而已。

translations.literal: Gaozu’s time then Liu Jing—Empress Lü’s time Fan Kuai, Ji Bu—Emperor Xiaowen’s time Jia Yi, Chao Cuo—Emperor Xiaowu’s time Wang Hui, Han Anguo, Zhu Maichen, Gongsun Hong, Dong Zhongshu—each held seen views—each had same difference—yet gather their essentials—return two categories only.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Jing under Gaozu, Fan Kuai and Ji Bu under Empress Lü, Jia Yi and Chao Cuo under Wen, Wang Hui, Han Anguo, Zhu Maichen, Gongsun Hong, and Dong Zhongshu under Wu—each voiced a view, yet all reduce to two camps.

sentences.zh: 縉紳之儒則守和親，介冑之士則言征伐，皆偏見一時之利害，而未究匈奴之終始也。

translations.literal: Sash-girded scholars then uphold peace kinship—armored knights then speak campaign strike—all biased view one moment’s benefit harm—yet not exhaust Xiongnu’s beginning end.
translations.idiomatic: Gowned scholars cling to heqin while mailed warriors demand war—both chase momentary gain without mastering the Xiongnu arc.

sentences.zh: 自漢興以至於今，曠世歷年，多於春秋，其與匈奴，有修文而和親之矣，有用武而克伐之矣，有卑下而承事之矣，有威服而臣畜之矣，詘伸異變，強弱相反，是故其詳可得而言也。

translations.literal: Since Han arose reaching today—empty generations passing years—more than Spring and Autumn—with Xiongnu—there was cultivate culture thereby peace kinship—there was use arms thereby conquer strike—there was humble low thereby undertake service—there was awe submit thereby minister herd—bend stretch different changes—strong weak opposite turn—therefore its details can obtain thereby speak.
translations.idiomatic: Han has endured longer than the Spring and Autumn era; with the Xiongnu it has shifted from cultured peace to martial conquest, from humble service to awe and vassalage—flexing strength and weakness—so the story can be told in full.

sentences.zh: 昔和親之論，發於劉敬。

translations.literal: Formerly peace kinship discourse—issued from Liu Jing.
translations.idiomatic: The heqin debate began with Liu Jing.

sentences.zh: 是時，天下初定，新遭平城之難，故從其言，約結和親，賂遺單于，冀以救安邊境。

translations.literal: At this time—All-under-Heaven newly settled—newly suffered Pingcheng calamity—therefore followed his words—covenant tied peace kinship—bribes gifted Chanyu—hoped thereby rescue pacify border.
translations.idiomatic: The realm had just endured Pingcheng, so court adopted his counsel—treaties, brides, and gifts to buy quiet marches.

sentences.zh: 孝惠、高後時遵而不違，匈奴寇盜不為衰止，而單於反以加驕倨。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaohui, Empress Gao times obeyed yet not violated—Xiongnu raiding stealing not thereby decline stop—yet Chanyu contrary thereby increased arrogant haughty.
translations.idiomatic: Under Hui and the Empress Dowager policy continued yet raids never slackened—the Chanyu only grew haughtier.

sentences.zh: 逮至孝文，與通關市，妻以漢女，增厚其賂，歲以千金，而匈奴數背約束，邊境屢被其害。

translations.literal: Reaching Emperor Xiaowen—with opened barrier markets—wived with Han girls—increased thick their bribes—yearly with thousand gold—yet Xiongnu repeatedly betrayed covenants—border repeatedly suffered their harm.
translations.idiomatic: Wen opened markets, married princesses north, and poured out a thousand gold yearly—yet the Xiongnu broke every pact and plagued the frontier.

sentences.zh: 是以文帝中年，赫然發憤，遂躬戎服，親御鞍馬，從六郡良家材力之士，馳射上林，講習戰陳，聚天下精兵，軍於廣武，顧問馮唐，與論將帥，喟然歎息，思古名臣。

translations.literal: Therefore Emperor Wen middle years—brilliantly roused anger—thereupon personally martial dress—personally drove saddle horse—from six commanderies good-house talented strength knights—galloped shot Shanglin—drilled studied battle formations—gathered All-under-Heaven elite troops—armied at Guangwu—consulted Feng Tang—with discussed generals marshals—sighed long lament—thought ancient famed ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Mid-reign Wen donned armor, rode with picked youths from six commanderies, drilled at Shanglin, massed crack troops at Guangwu, questioned Feng Tang on commanders, and sighed for heroes past.

sentences.zh: 此則和親無益，已然之明效也。

translations.literal: This then peace kinship without benefit—already thus clear proof.
translations.idiomatic: Thus heqin had proved useless beyond doubt.

sentences.zh: 仲舒親見四世之事，猶復欲守舊文，頗增其約。

translations.literal: Zhongshu personally saw four generations’ affairs—still again wished guard old texts—somewhat increased its covenants.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu witnessed four reigns yet still clung to antique formulas with tighter pledges.

sentences.zh: 以為：「義動君子，利動貪人。

translations.literal: Thought: ‘Duty moves gentlemen—profit moves greedy men.’
translations.idiomatic: He argued that righteousness moves gentlemen while profit moves the greedy.’

sentences.zh: 如匈奴者，非可以仁義說也，獨可說以厚利，結之於天耳。

translations.literal: Such as Xiongnu—not can by benevolent duty persuade—only can persuade by thick profit—knotted with Heaven ears.’
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu heed neither ritual nor humanity—only thick bribes and oaths sworn before Heaven.’

sentences.zh: 故與之厚利以沒其意，與盟於天以堅其約，質其愛子以累其心，匈奴雖欲展轉，奈失重利何，奈欺上天何，奈殺愛子何！

translations.literal: Therefore give them thick profit thereby sink their intent—with covenant at Heaven thereby firm their covenant—hostage their beloved son thereby burden their heart—Xiongnu though wish twist-turn—how lose heavy profit—how deceive High Heaven—how kill beloved son!’
translations.idiomatic: Feed them profit to dull ambition, seal oaths under Heaven, hold their heir hostage—how could they twist away profit, Heaven, or a beloved son?’

sentences.zh: 夫賦斂行賂不足以當三軍之費，城郭之固無以異於貞士之約，而使邊城守境之民父兄緩帶，稚子咽哺，胡馬不窺於長城，而羽檄不行於中國，不亦便於天下乎！

translations.literal: As for levies bribes not sufficient match three armies’ expense—walled cities’ solidity no difference from steadfast knight’s covenant—yet order border cities guard territory people fathers elder-brothers slack belts—young children suckle nurse—Hu horses not peer Great Wall—yet feather dispatches not travel Central States—is this not convenient All-under-Heaven!’
translations.idiomatic: It costs less than armies or walls yet lets frontier folk loosen belts while babes nurse undisturbed—no Hu scouts at the Long Wall and no emergency runners inland—is that not ease for the realm!’

sentences.zh: 」察仲舒之論，考諸行事，乃知其未合於當時，而有闕於後世也。

translations.literal: Examine Zhongshu’s discourse—investigate various conducted affairs—then know it not matched present age—yet lacked toward later generations.
translations.idiomatic: Measured against events his scheme failed its own age and left gaps for posterity.

sentences.zh: 當孝武時，雖征伐克獲，而士馬物故亦略相當；

translations.literal: When Emperor Xiaowu—though campaigning conquered captures—yet soldiers horses casualties also roughly matched;
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wu victories matched losses in men and mounts;

sentences.zh: 雖開河南之野，建朔方之郡，亦棄造陽之北九百餘里。

translations.literal: though opened south-of-River wilds—built Shuofang commandery—also abandoned north of Zaoyang nine hundred odd li.
translations.idiomatic: Han gained Henan and Shuofang yet surrendered nine hundred li north of Zaoyang.

sentences.zh: 匈奴人民每來降漢，單于亦輒拘留漢使以相報復，其桀驁尚如斯，安肯以愛子而為質乎？

translations.literal: Xiongnu people whenever came surrender Han—Chanyu also always detain Han envoys thereby mutual revenge—their fierce arrogance still thus—how willing use beloved son thereby hostage?
translations.idiomatic: Whenever Han welcomed defectors the Chanyu seized envoys in revenge—such arrogance—would they truly surrender an heir?

sentences.zh: 此不合當時之言也。

translations.literal: This not matched present-age speech.
translations.idiomatic: Thus his counsel misread the moment.

sentences.zh: 若不置質，空約和親，是襲孝文既往之悔，而長匈奴無已之詐也。

translations.literal: If not place hostage—empty covenant peace kinship—this repeats Emperor Wen past regret—yet lengthen Xiongnu unending deceit.
translations.idiomatic: Hostage-free heqin repeats Wen’s old regret and feeds endless nomad deceit.

sentences.zh: 夫邊城不選守境武略之臣，修障隧備塞之具，厲長戟勁弩之械，恃吾所以待邊寇而務賦斂於民，遠行貨賂，割剝百姓，以奉寇讎。

translations.literal: As for border cities not select guard territory martial strategy ministers—repair barriers beacons prepare pass gear—sharpen long halberd strong crossbow weapons—rely our means await border raiders yet rush levies on people—far travel goods bribes—strip peel hundred surnames—thereby serve raider foes.
translations.idiomatic: Without picking frontier commanders, repairing walls and weapons, and trusting defense, grinding taxes to ship bribes abroad bleeds the people to enrich the enemy.

sentences.zh: 信甘言，守空約，而幾胡馬之不窺，不已過乎！

translations.literal: Trust sweet words—guard empty covenant—yet nearly Hu horses not spy—is this not excessive!
translations.idiomatic: Sweet talk and empty treaties hardly keep Hu horses from the wall—is that not folly!

sentences.zh: 至孝宣之世，承武帝奮擊之威，直匈奴百年之運，因其壞亂幾亡之厄，權時施宜，覆以威德，然後單于稽首臣服，遣子入侍，三世稱籓，賓於漢庭。

translations.literal: Reaching Emperor Xiaoxuan’s age—inherited Emperor Wu fierce strike’s awe—straight Xiongnu hundred-year fortune—because their ruined chaos near-perished calamity—weighed time applied fitting—cover with awe virtue—afterward Chanyu knocked head submit serve—sent son enter attendance—three generations titled screen—guest at Han court.
translations.idiomatic: Xuan inherited Wu’s momentum, exploited nomad collapse, blended awe with kindness until the Chanyu kowtowed, sent heirs, and for three reigns served as vassal guest at Han court.

sentences.zh: 是時，邊城晏閉，牛馬布野，三世無犬吠之警，黎庶亡干戈之役。

translations.literal: At this time—border cities calmly closed—cattle horses spread wild—three generations without dog-bark alarms—black-headed folk without shield-spear service.
translations.idiomatic: Gates shut peacefully; herds carpeted the grass—three generations heard no night alarms and commoners bore no arms.

sentences.zh: 後六十餘載之間，遭王莽篡位，始開邊隙，單于由是歸怨自絕，莽遂斬其侍子，邊境之禍構矣。

translations.literal: After sixty odd carrying-years-between—suffered Wang Mang usurp—first opened border crack—Chanyu thereby returned resentment self-cut—Mang then beheaded his hostage son—border calamity constructed.
translations.idiomatic: Six decades later Wang Mang’s coup reopened the rift; the Chanyu broke away in wrath and Mang executed his hostage—frontier disaster followed.

sentences.zh: 故呼韓邪始朝於漢，漢議其儀，而蕭望之曰：「戎狄荒服，言其來服荒忽無常，時至時去，宜待以客禮，讓而不臣。

translations.literal: Therefore Huhanye first courted Han—Han deliberated his ritual—yet Xiao Wangzhi said: ‘Rong-Di wild submission—speaks their coming submit wild blur inconstant—sometimes arrive sometimes depart—fitting treat with guest ritual—defer yet not subject.’
translations.idiomatic: When Huhanye first came Han debated rites; Xiao Wangzhi urged treating nomads as wandering guests—deferential but not subjects.’

sentences.zh: 如其後嗣遁逃竄伏，使於中國不為叛臣。

translations.literal: If their later heirs flee hide crawl conceal—send mission Central States not become rebel ministers.’
translations.idiomatic: If heirs flee, Han envoys need not count them rebels.’

sentences.zh: 」及孝元時，議罷守塞之備，侯應以為不可，可謂盛不忘衰，安必思危，遠見識微之明矣。

translations.literal: Reaching Emperor Xiaoyuan times—deliberated dismiss guard-pass preparations—Hou Ying deemed not possible—can say flourishing not forget decline—peace surely think peril—far sight discern subtle brilliance.
translations.idiomatic: Under Yuan, Hou Ying’s veto of stripping the wall showed prosperity mindful of peril—far-sighted caution.

sentences.zh: 至單于鹹棄其愛子，昧利不顧，侵掠所獲，歲巨萬計，而和親賂遺，不過千金，安在其不棄質而失重利也？

translations.literal: Reaching Chanyu Xian discarded his beloved son—blind profit not heed—raid plunder captures—year counted in tens of thousands—yet peace kinship bribes—not exceed thousand gold—where lies his not discard hostage yet lose heavy profit?
translations.idiomatic: Chanyu Xian traded sons for countless raid loot while heqin gifts topped out at a thousand gold—where was Dong’s hostage leverage?

sentences.zh: 仲舒之言，漏於是矣。

translations.literal: Zhongshu’s words—leaked herein.
translations.idiomatic: Here Dong Zhongshu’s doctrine leaks.

sentences.zh: 夫規事建議，不圖萬世之固，而偷恃一時之事者，未可以經遠也。

translations.literal: As for planning affairs raising counsel—not chart ten thousand generations’ solidity—yet stealth rely one moment’s affair—cannot thereby traverse far.
translations.idiomatic: Policy that chases quick fixes instead of lasting foundations cannot endure.

sentences.zh: 若乃征伐之功，秦、漢行事，嚴尤論之當矣。

translations.literal: As for campaign achievements—Qin Han conduct affairs—Yan You discussed them apt.
translations.idiomatic: On warfare Qin–Han practice—Yan You judged rightly.

sentences.zh: 故先王度土，中立封畿，分九州，列五服，物土貢，制外內，或修刑政，或昭文德，遠近之勢異也。

translations.literal: Therefore former kings measured soil—centrally established enfeoffed capital—divided nine provinces—arrayed five zones—assigned soil tribute—regulated outer inner—some cultivated punishments government—some displayed culture virtue—far near’s situation differs.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient kings surveyed the realm, ringed the capital, carved nine provinces and five tribute zones, fixing inner from outer—law or culture—according to distance.

sentences.zh: 是以《春秋》內諸夏而外夷狄，夷狄之人貪而好利，被發左衽，人而獸心，其與中國殊章服，異習俗，飲食不同，言語不通，辟居北垂寒露之野，逐草隨畜，射獵為生，隔以山谷，雍以沙幕，天地所以絕外內地。

translations.literal: Therefore Spring and Autumn inner Zhongxia outer Yi-Di—Yi-Di people greedy yet fond profit—dishevel hair left lapel—human yet beast heart—they with Central States differ insignia dress—differ customs habits—drink food not same—speech not penetrate—dwell apart north fringe cold-dew wilds—chase grass follow livestock—shoot hunt as life—divided by mountains valleys—blocked by sand desert—Heaven Earth thereby sever outer inner lands.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn seats the Hua within and barbarians without—nomads are greedy, wild-haired, beast-hearted, alien in dress, diet, and tongue; they roam the northern wastes following herds, separated by ravines and desert—the cosmic divide between inner and outer.

sentences.zh: 是故聖王禽獸畜之，不與約誓，不就攻伐；

translations.literal: Therefore sage kings bird-beast herd them—not with covenant oath—not undertake attack strike;
translations.idiomatic: Sages herd them like beasts—no binding oaths, no eager strikes;

sentences.zh: 約之則費賂而見欺，攻之則勞師而招寇。

translations.literal: Covenant them then expense bribes yet seen deceive—strike them then weary armies yet summon raiders.
translations.idiomatic: treaties bleed treasure yet invite betrayal; campaigns weary troops yet summon reprisals.

sentences.zh: 其地不可耕而食也，其民不可臣而畜也，是以外而不內，疏而不戚，政教不及其人，正朔不加其國；

translations.literal: Their lands cannot plow thereby eat—their people cannot minister thereby herd—therefore outer yet not inner—distant yet not kin—government teaching not reach their persons—correct calendar not apply their states;
translations.idiomatic: Their soil cannot feed Middle Kingdom farmers nor their folk become subjects—keep them outside the kinship circle; decrees and calendars do not cross their borders;

sentences.zh: 來則懲而御之，去則備而守之。

translations.literal: Come then punish thereby ward—depart then prepare thereby guard.
translations.idiomatic: strike when they come; dig in when they leave.

sentences.zh: 其慕義而貢獻，則接之以禮讓，羈靡不絕，使曲在彼，蓋聖王制御蠻夷之常道也。

translations.literal: Their admiring righteousness thereby tribute offering—then receive them with ritual deference—bridle halter unbroken—order bent lies there—cover sage kings regulate control barbarians’ constant way.
translations.idiomatic: When they seek righteousness with gifts, greet them courteously and keep loose reins so blame stays theirs—such is the sage king’s standing rule for barbarians.
